CIPHER.1354.cipherString: In repaying kindness, we ought to imitate fertile lands, which give back much more than they have received.
CIPHER.1354.question: Marcus Tullius Cicero has been heard to say the following phrase that has been encoded using the Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.1354.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero

CIPHER.1355.cipherString: Genius worship is the inevitable sign of an uncreative age.
CIPHER.1355.question: You receive this cryptic message from the future through an online time capsule. It's a quote by Clive Bell, written in an Atbash. What does it say?
CIPHER.1355.author: Clive Bell

CIPHER.1356.cipherString: This world, the whole of the planet called earth, is the common country of all who live and breathe upon it.
CIPHER.1356.question: The message below was intercepted from an experimental satellite transmission. It contains a quote by Desiderius Erasmus, encoded as an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.1356.author: Desiderius Erasmus

CIPHER.1357.cipherString: If you would know strength and patience, welcome the company of trees.
CIPHER.1357.question: Your science fair project logs contain a mysterious string of letters. It's a quote by Hal Borland, encoded as a Porta using a keyword of MIGHTY. What does it say?
CIPHER.1357.author: Hal Borland

CIPHER.1358.cipherString: From the height of these pyramids forty centuries look down on us.
CIPHER.1358.question: A fragment of text is discovered in a time capsule from the year 1800. It's a quote by Napoleon encrypted as a Nihilist Substitution Cipher with a keyword of SANDS and polybius key of DRAMATICALLY. What does it say?
CIPHER.1358.author: Napoleon

CIPHER.1359.cipherString: five out of four people have trouble with fractions.
CIPHER.1359.question: Your lab partner discovered this pattern on the back of a graphing calculator. It's a quote, encoded as a Baconian. What does it say?

CIPHER.1360.cipherString: An athlete cannot run with money in his pockets. He must run with hope in his heart and dreams in his head.
CIPHER.1360.question: Solve this quote by Emil Zatopek that has been encoded using the Caesar Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.1360.author: Emil Zatopek

CIPHER.1361.cipherString: It usually takes me two or three days to prepare an impromptu speech.
CIPHER.1361.question: Upon searching a room, the following were found on scraps of paper. You are told it's a quote by Mark Twain encoded as a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword length of 5. The 42nd through 50th cipher units (35 53 33 33 58 55 22 67 36 89) decode to be IMPROMPTU S. What does it say?
CIPHER.1361.author: Mark Twain

CIPHER.1362.cipherString: Never do card tricks for the group you play poker with.
CIPHER.1362.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a quote, encoded using a Fractionated Morse Cipher. You are told that the quote has E GROUP in it corresponding to the encoded text O A E Q H J L Q D, meaning A = ●××; D = –●×; E = ––●; H = ●×–; J = ––×; L = ●●–; O = ●●×; Q = ×●–. What does it say?
CIPHER.1362.author: Unknown

CIPHER.1363.cipherString: America is inching towards the metric system
CIPHER.1363.question: A mysterious balloon release at the science fair carried this Baconian banner. You are told that the cipher text ENDED WHALE CRIME DRAMA decodes to be INCH. What does it say?

CIPHER.1364.cipherString: Por la tarde, visitamos a un pueblo pintoresco. Descendimos a las profundidades de la tierra y exploramos sus cavas misteriosas.
CIPHER.1364.question: This Spanish-language entry from someone's travel journal has been encoded as a K1 Xenocrypt for you to solve.

CIPHER.1365.question: The morning announcements ended with a proverb encrypted in an Affine cipher with a=7 and b=8. What does it say?
CIPHER.1365.author: Proverb

CIPHER.1366.cipherString: Economists are tellers of stories and makers of poems.
CIPHER.1366.question: This coded note is discovered inside a sealed envelope labeled “Confidential.” It's a quote, encoded using a Baconian Cipher. What does it say?

CIPHER.1367.cipherString: I think of life as a good book. The further you get into it, the more it begins to make sense.
CIPHER.1367.question: A mysterious app update added this Checkerboard lock screen—it's actually Harold Kushner's quote with a polybius key of OPTIMIZATION. What does it say?
CIPHER.1367.author: Harold Kushner

CIPHER.1368.cipherString: Books have a unique way of stopping time in a particular moment and saying: Let's not forget this.
CIPHER.1368.question: A weathered note is found inside an old science textbook. The writing appears to be a quote by Dave Eggers encoded as an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.1368.author: Dave Eggers

CIPHER.1369.question: A message flashes briefly on a malfunctioning computer screen. It turns out to be a quote written in a Cryptarithm. What do the values 054397409: 104234 8115 decode to?

CIPHER.1370.cipherString: Risks must be taken because the greatest hazard in life is to risk nothing.
CIPHER.1370.question: Frozen inside an ice core sample from Antarctica: this Atbash message by Leo F. Buscaglia. What does it say?
CIPHER.1370.author: Leo F. Buscaglia

CIPHER.1371.cipherString: Life is made up of marble and mud.
CIPHER.1371.question: Found inside a hollowed-out periodic table book: this Affine Cipher hides Nathaniel Hawthorne's insight. You are told that the cipher text XM decodes to be OF. What does it say?
CIPHER.1371.author: Nathaniel Hawthorne

CIPHER.1372.cipherString: A compromise is the art of dividing a cake in such a way that everyone believes he has the biggest piece.
CIPHER.1372.question: Ludwig Erhard offers an observation that has been encoded as a Caesar cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.1372.author: Ludwig Erhard

CIPHER.1373.cipherString: Luego fuimos de tapas y descansamos un poco porque había que esperar la puesta de sol y la oscuridad.
CIPHER.1373.question: Here is part of someone's Spanish-language travel journal, encoded as a K1 Xenocrypt with an English keyword. What does it say?

CIPHER.1374.question: You receive an anonymous text message containing a coded passage. It's said to be a phrase encrypted with a Cryptarithm. What do the values 900925 6281176 45451 decode to?

CIPHER.1375.cipherString: If you can't write a decent short story because of the cold, write something else. Write anything. Write a long letter to somebody.
CIPHER.1375.question: William Saroyan offers us some advice about being an author that has been encoded as a Patristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains A LONG, corresponding to the ciphertext S BEDY on the last line. What does it say?
CIPHER.1375.author: William Saroyan

CIPHER.1376.cipherString: If an author would have us feel a strong degree of compassion, his characters must not be too perfect.
CIPHER.1376.question: A mysterious postcard arrives from an unknown sender. It contains a coded quote by Anna Letitia Barbauld, written in a Complete Columnar. The quote has WOULD somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.1376.author: Anna Letitia Barbauld

CIPHER.1377.cipherString: To forgive is to erase a past, depriving it of its power to stain the present.
CIPHER.1377.question: You find this encoded line written in invisible ink on a lab safety poster. It's a quote by Yi-Fu Tuan, written in a Porta. The 33rd through 39th cipher characters (XAAOVAD) decode to be IT OF ITS. What does it say?
CIPHER.1377.author: Yi-Fu Tuan

CIPHER.1378.cipherString: It was like the effect of the light of the moon on water, and perfume on the air, and a lavender field on the wind.
CIPHER.1378.question: You came across the following written on a wall in a cave. You are told that it's a quote by Najwa Bin Shatwan encoded as an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.1378.author: Najwa Bin Shatwan

CIPHER.1379.cipherString: It takes wisdom, power and influence to be able to make a positive impact on what goes on in society and in the nation as a whole.
CIPHER.1379.question: Found on a microchip inside a disassembled robotics kit: this Patristocrat Cipher about helpfulness by Sunday Adelaja using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains POWER corresponding with the ciphertext letters NMTIZ on the first line. What does it say?
CIPHER.1379.author: Sunday Adelaja

CIPHER.1380.cipherString: The truth is like salt. Men want to taste a little, but too much makes everyone sick.
CIPHER.1380.question: Etched into the bottom of the cafeteria's mystery-cookie tray: this Checkerboard quote by Joe Abercrombie. The 19th through 27th cipher units (CK LK NC HA UA NC UK UK US) decode to be MENWANTTO. What does it say?
CIPHER.1380.author: Joe Abercrombie

CIPHER.1381.cipherString: I think football would become an even better game if someone could invent a ball that kicks back.
CIPHER.1381.question: Someone left this encrypted quote scribbled on the chalkboard before class began. It's a quote, written as a K1 Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.1381.author: Unknown

CIPHER.1382.cipherString: Not everybody goes to movies to get their life changed.
CIPHER.1382.question: A glitchy hologram in the school planetarium displays this Fractionated Morse version of Samuel L. Jackson's words. You are told that the quote has MOVIE in it corresponding to the encoded text K J P E B A, meaning A = ●×●; B = ●●×; E = ●–×; J = –––; K = ––×; P = ×●●. What does it say?
CIPHER.1382.author: Samuel L. Jackson

CIPHER.1383.cipherString: People built homes the way they bought eggs: a little at a time.
CIPHER.1383.question: You stumble across this encoded text on an old floppy disk labeled “Project Discovery.” It's a quote by Trevor Noah encoded as a Complete Columnar. The quote has HOMES THE somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.1383.author: Trevor Noah
